goal: verify that a real CLI-backed failure path stops after worker2, persists a failed execute result, and does not create commit, PR, or deliver side effects
steps: Create a disposable workspace copy for the repo runtime.
Start the demo in the background and wait for healthcheck success.
POST a normal execute intake task that should reach the CLI runner.
Allow the worker pipeline to process the task and wait until the execute task settles.
Inspect logs, persisted task rows, token usage rows, and any mock SCM side effects.
Clean up background processes before finishing.
inputs: intake payload for a tiny implementation task that should reach the real CLI runner
assertions: The CLI runner is invoked through the real runtime path.
The selected trigger is evaluated honestly: if it exits non-zero, the execute task ends in failed status and the persisted execute result records a failed CLI run.
If the selected trigger still exits zero in this environment, the scenario must be reported as failed with preserved evidence instead of claiming the failure branch was verified.
When a real non-zero exit occurs, no deliver task is created.
When a real non-zero exit occurs, no mock commit or PR is created after the failed run.
When a real non-zero exit occurs, no token usage rows are written for the failed run.
collect: evidence showing whether the chosen trigger produced a real non-zero exit

context.title: Real CLI non-zero exit verification
context.description: Trigger a real CLI-backed failure path.
acceptance_criteria: Execution failure is recorded without downstream delivery side effects

input_payload.instructions: Try to implement a tiny change so the runtime reaches the CLI runner.
